nodes.label: Primary Learning Objective
nodes.description: Full description of what the learner should be able to do (optional — used in agent context and tooltips)
nodes.win_condition: One explicit sentence describing what success looks like, written for an AI (optional — used as the assessment standard and shown on the evidence map). Leave no tacit word like 'strong' or 'clear' undefined.
misconceptions: Common misconception #1 (optional — used by the agent to probe for gaps)

nodes.label: Foundation Concept
nodes.description: A prerequisite concept that supports the learning objective
nodes.win_condition: The learner succeeds when they can [do the specific, observable thing that proves they've grasped this concept].
misconceptions: A typical student misunderstanding about this concept

nodes.label: Intermediate Concept
nodes.description: A concept that builds on the foundation

nodes.label: Applied Skill
nodes.description: A practical skill that requires understanding of the concepts above

edges.description: Must understand the foundation before the intermediate concept (optional)
This concept is required for the learning objective
The foundation concept enables this applied skill
The skill builds on the learning objective
